content:
{
  "title": "Adversarial Entanglement Prompts: Dynamic Multi-Context Prompting to Boost LLM Robustness",
  "problem": "LLMs remain vulnerable to sophisticated jailbreaks and adversarial prompts that can bypass static or single-turn defenses. Existing methods often rely on one-shot prompts, fixed shield structures, or post-attack detection, leaving models exposed to multi-turn and context-shifting attacks.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are deployed in increasingly sensitive applications, robust defenses against adversarial prompt engineering are critical. Attackers are escalating to multi-turn and context-based jailbreaks, which current prompt-based defenses and benchmarks do not adequately address. A new approach is needed to proactively entangle safe contexts and disrupt adversarial trajectories during inference.",
  "method": "We propose Adversarial Entanglement Prompts: a dynamic prompting method that, at each dialogue turn, interleaves adversarially-generated safe context sub-prompts with user instructions. These sub-prompts are sampled or synthesized to target recent dialog history and are designed to steer the model toward safe completions, even under adversarial manipulation. The method leverages an auxiliary context generator adversarially trained to anticipate jailbreak strategies, thus entangling benign intent within future context windows and thwarting multi-turn attacks. This operates at inference time, without model fine-tuning.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on JailbreakBench and AdvBench, along with real-world multi-turn adversarial prompt datasets. Baselines include static shield prompting and AdaShield. Metrics include attack success rate, safe completion rate, and conversational utility. Ablation studies will test the effectiveness of adaptive context entanglement versus static and single-turn prompts."
}
